title: Derech Eretz: Good Manners Before Torah
title_zh: 德里奇·艾雷茨：礼仪先于律法
title_he: דרך ארץ: דרך ארץ קדמה לתורה
cultural_parallels: Confucian Li (礼) - ritual propriety and proper conduct
Chinese filial piety and respect for elders
Eastern Han Dynasty (东汉, 25-220 CE) - contemporary with Mishnaic period
Confucius and Spring/Autumn period parallels with First Temple period
Integration of learning (学) and moral practice (行)

historical_context.chinese_equivalent: Warring States through Han Dynasty (战国-汉朝)

sources: Mishneh Torah, Laws of Character Traits
themes: Character development as prerequisite for Torah study
Cross-cultural wisdom: Jewish and Confucian parallels
Integration of practical wisdom and sacred learning
Universal values vs. particular religious practices
Proper conduct in business and daily life
target_audience: Mandarin speakers with no Jewish background, Chinese Jews, converts to Judaism in Asia
author: Torah Light Writing Team
header_image_style: Chinese shan shui (山水) ink wash landscape with scholarly courtyard garden scene
